tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting significant renal impairment with possible nephrotic-range proteinuria and impaired concentrating ability. Low HbA1c with glucosuria and ketonuria may indicate recent onset or poorly controlled diabetes or another metabolic disturbance. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may suggest underlying urinary tract pathology. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
